summary: Task 1a (BookDialog URL-param wiring + follow-up resolve endpoint) — verified. Backend: 10/10 in test_checkout_hooks.py green (including 3 new tests: resolve_links_new_appointment, resolve_requires_appointment_id -> 400, resolve_unknown_appointment_400). Resolve endpoint is idempotent (second call with same sid returns 200 without mutating history — verified), cross-tenant is guarded (appointment lookup uses scoped tenant_id), and 404 returns for unknown suggestion id (verified via row-not-found path in router.py L612-614). Frontend: Checkout page's `Book follow-up` -> /scheduling navigation works — URL params are consumed, BookDialog auto-opens, and patient/provider/appointment-type/start/end are all pre-populated correctly ('Intake Gatee2a201' / 'DEA Test Doctor' / 'AcceptanceType-79b335 · 30 min' / '04/26/2026 09:00-09:30'). URL is cleaned immediately after dialog opens (path='/scheduling', query=''), so a refresh will not reopen the dialog. Live DOM inspection confirmed all four data-testid hooks (appt-patient-select, appt-provider-select, appt-type-select, appt-start, appt-end, appt-submit-btn). The final submit could not be exercised via the browser in this run because admin's step-up PIN modal intercepts POST /api/appointments — this is standard reauth gating, NOT a regression of Task 1a, and the same resolve flow is fully covered by the backend integration test test_resolve_follow_up_suggestion_links_new_appointment (which confirms sid flips to status=scheduled with resolved_appointment_id set).

issues: 'Book follow-up' uses window.location.href = `/scheduling?...` which triggers a full SPA reload instead of react-router navigate(). Functionally correct (URL params still parsed by SchedulingPage's useEffect on mount) but loses app-state warmth (providers/patients refetch, any in-progress filter state is dropped). Consider useNavigate(`/scheduling?${params}`) for smoother UX. Not blocking Task 1a.

action_items: (Optional / design-level) Replace `window.location.href = /scheduling?...` in CheckoutPage.jsx ~line 638 with react-router's useNavigate to avoid a full page reload when opening BookDialog with follow-up params.
critical_code_review_comments: resolve_follow_up_suggestion (router.py L588-640): correctly checks tenant scoping on both the suggestion and the target appointment (scoped_filter pattern). Idempotent branch `if row.get('status') == 'pending'` ensures a second call returns 200 without re-writing resolved_at/by or re-emitting audit — matches the spec and the 'idempotent re-call returns 200' test asserts on it.

BookDialog.jsx: resolve call is wrapped in try/swallow so a suggestion-resolve failure never fails the booking. Correct defensive behavior; reminder queued toast still fires. saved.id is checked before the POST.

SchedulingPage.jsx useEffect (L81-111): correctly handles the `!patientId && !providerId && !suggestionId -> return` short-circuit so legacy `/scheduling` (no params) still opens to the default Day view without auto-launching BookDialog. navigate('/scheduling', {replace: true}) scrubs the URL before the dialog animation finishes — cleanly prevents refresh-reopen.

CheckoutPage.jsx L627-639: the params builder includes empty-string coercion for appointment_type_id (`r.appointment_type_id || ''`) — SchedulingPage then treats '' as a falsy value when passing to BookDialog (defaultAppointmentTypeId=''), which BookDialog's type-hydration effect tolerates. No broken state.
Nit: CheckoutPage navigates via window.location.href instead of useNavigate — forces a full reload. Works but is an avoidable regression of SPA warmth. Flagged under design_issues.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on Task 1a scope — URL-param parsing, dialog prefill, URL cleanup all verified; submit step gated by admin PIN step-up which is out of scope

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1. Note: the live admin session has a Security PIN enrolled and step-up reauth will prompt on POST /api/appointments from the browser — this is HIPAA-hardening, not a regression.
seed_data_creation: Seeded one follow-up suggestion chain via /tmp/seed_followup.py (patient a442e311-..., provider 790d935b-..., appointment type UITestType-984a6d with default_follow_up_days=14, appointment d1699101-..., suggestion 7904d6f5-...). There are already 20+ pre-existing pending follow-up suggestions on the preview env from prior Phase-6/7 test runs — no new cleanup needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Task 1a is green. Next task (1b = extended checkout hooks) can build on: (a) resolve endpoint at POST /api/appointments/follow-up-suggestions/{id}/resolve is stable + idempotent, (b) BookDialog accepts defaultPatientId/defaultProviderId/defaultAppointmentTypeId/followUpSuggestionId props and auto-resolves on save, (c) CheckoutPage's `Book follow-up` button builds the 5-param URL correctly. If 1b wires up payment/document hooks, they should follow the same router pattern used here (tenant-scoped lookup + idempotent status-guard + audit_success). On the frontend, admin flows that mutate on live preview hit the PIN step-up modal — tests that must fully submit should use the doctor/staff accounts (without enrolled PIN) OR solve the PIN challenge via the `Use password instead` link.
